mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns show a positive mean during periods of rising FEDFUNDS, or the rank correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and forward returns falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.2pp threshold, or the signal loses predictive power with an IC below 0.01 during periods of stable employment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive movement of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.5 or a mean forward return below 0.1% per month.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days when the economy is in a confirmed expansion phase.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during expansion phases, or the predictive IC drops below 0.015 in a 24-month rolling test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 2.5% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are non-negative in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5% threshold, or the signal shows no statistical significance with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and aggregate demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or the mean return during payroll growth months falls below 0.05%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and monthly retail sales growth confirm robust consumer demand fundamentals, which drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved earnings expectations.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered periods, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside accelerating inflation indicates supply-side bottlenecks and margin compression, which generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of monetary policy uncertainty.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with a positive mean forward return exceeding 0.2% during stagflation risk periods, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant with a p-value above 0.05.
